data.title: Bitcoin ETFs Surge: $1B in U.S., $2B in Canada, Europe Follows
data.description:
Bitcoin ETFs have been stealing the limelight from gold in recent months, as investors increasingly turn to the digital asset as a safe haven and store of value. The launch of the first U.S. Bitcoin ETF, ProShares Bitcoin Strategy ETF (BITO), in October 2021, marked a significant milestone in the cryptocurrency's journey towards mainstream acceptance.

The ETF, which tracks the performance of Bitcoin futures contracts, has seen remarkable success since its debut. As of February 2022, BITO has amassed over $1 billion in assets under management, making it one of the largest and most liquid Bitcoin investment vehicles available to U.S. investors.

Bitcoin ETFs have also been gaining traction in other regions. In Canada, the Purpose Bitcoin ETF (BTCC) and the Evolve Bitcoin ETF (EBIT) have been popular among investors, with combined assets under management exceeding $2 billion. In Europe, the 21Shares Bitcoin ETP (ABTC) and the CoinShares Physical Bitcoin (BTCX) have also attracted significant investment.

The growing popularity of Bitcoin ETFs can be attributed to several factors. Firstly, ETFs provide investors with an easy and convenient way to gain exposure to Bitcoin without the need to hold the underlying asset. Secondly, ETFs offer greater liquidity and diversification benefits compared to direct investments in Bitcoin. Lastly, ETFs are subject to regulatory oversight, which can help to alleviate some of the concerns surrounding the volatility and security of cryptocurrencies.

However, the rise of Bitcoin ETFs has not been without controversy. Some market participants have raised concerns about the potential for manipulation in the Bitcoin futures market, which underlies many Bitcoin ETFs. Additionally, there have been debates about the environmental impact of Bitcoin mining, which has led some investors to question the sustainability of the cryptocurrency.

Despite these challenges, Bitcoin ETFs continue to attract significant investment, as investors seek to diversify their portfolios and hedge against inflation. As the cryptocurrency market matures and gains wider acceptance, it is likely that we will see further innovation in Bitcoin ETFs and other investment vehicles, which could help to drive even greater adoption of the digital asset.

data.title: SEC Seeks Public Input on CoinShares' XRP and Litecoin ETFs
data.description:
The U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) has acknowledged filings from European asset manager CoinShares for the world's first U.S.-based spot XRP and Litecoin exchange-traded funds (ETFs). The regulator is now seeking public comments on the applications within 21 days of the filings' publication.

The proposed ETFs would track the performance of XRP, the third-largest cryptocurrency by market capitalization, and Litecoin, which ranks 15th. Both assets are among the oldest altcoins, having entered the market shortly after Bitcoin's launch in 2009.

CoinShares' applications come amidst a surge in demand for crypto ETFs, following the SEC's approval of 11 spot Bitcoin ETFs last year. These funds have attracted significant capital, with BlackRock's iShares Bitcoin Trust (IBIT) now managing about $55 billion in assets. Additionally, the SEC has approved eight Ethereum ETFs, signaling a shift in the regulator's stance towards crypto products.

The SEC's decision on CoinShares' XRP and Litecoin ETF filings will depend on the feedback received from the public and the regulator's internal review process. Last week, the Nasdaq filed 19b-4 forms with the SEC, requesting a rules change that would permit the two potential ETFs to list on the stock exchange. An S-1 registration statement must still receive the agency's greenlight, but such approvals are generally a formality.

CoinShares is not the only asset manager seeking the SEC's approval for new crypto ETFs. The growing demand for these investment products underscores issuers' efforts to cater to the voracious appetite for crypto-focused investment options among traditional investors.

data.title: "SEC Reviews CoinShares' ETF Proposals for XRP, Litecoin"
data.description:
The U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) has initiated a review of CoinShares' proposals to launch exchange-traded funds (ETFs) for XRP and Litecoin, marking a significant development in the cryptocurrency investment landscape. This move could open doors for mainstream investors to gain exposure to these major altcoins, reflecting an evolving regulatory stance.

CoinShares, a leading digital asset management firm, has submitted applications to the SEC for the first U.S.-based spot ETFs for XRP and Litecoin. This filing signals a growing inclination from the regulator to consider a broader array of crypto assets for ETF products, a shift from previous years characterized by stringent approvals. The anticipated ETFs aim to closely track the prices of these cryptocurrencies, providing investors with a regulated framework to participate in the crypto market.

Historically, both XRP and Litecoin have maintained their positions as prominent players in the cryptocurrency landscape. With XRP currently holding the third-largest market capitalization and Litecoin at the fifteenth, their proposed ETFs are poised to attract significant investor interest. The applications are expected to proceed through a standard comment period, allowing stakeholders a voice in the approval process. Investors are hopeful for a favorable outcome as the SEC seeks to adapt its regulatory approach to crypto market evolution.

The momentum behind cryptocurrency ETFs has surged markedly following the SEC’s approval of various Bitcoin ETFs last year. Traditional investors can now purchase shares in these funds that track the leading cryptocurrency, reflecting a seismic shift in investment strategy. CoinShares’ proposals signify a response to the compelling demand for diverse crypto investment vehicles beyond Bitcoin. The appetite for crypto ETFs appears insatiable, suggesting that if approved, these products could see substantial inflows quickly.

As the SEC deliberates, the broader crypto markets are showing positive signs, evidenced by Bitcoin trading at approximately $96,000. This uptick indicates a growing confidence among investors regarding future regulatory leniency. Analysts predict that the SEC may adopt a more supportive stance toward crypto products under the new administration, enhancing the chances for Elon Musk’s predicted “crypto-friendlier” policies to shape the market landscape. As the applications move forward, dedicated industry observers will closely watch the market’s reaction.

data.title: Nasdaq Proposes Rule Change to Boost Digital Asset Trading
data.description:
Nasdaq, a leading U.S.-based stock exchange, has proposed a new rule change that could significantly impact the digital asset investment landscape. The proposed rule, Nasdaq Rule 5712, aims to allow the listing and trading of digital asset-based investment interests, including securities issued by entities holding digital assets and investment products derived from commodities, cash, derivatives, or other sources.

The proposed rule change is particularly notable for its potential impact on the Hashdex Nasdaq Crypto Index US ETF (NCIQ). Currently listed under a different rule, the proposal envisions reclassifying the NCIQ under Rule 5712. The filing states that the proposal aims to amend representations regarding the investment objective and strategy of the NCIQ made in the rule change previously filed with and approved by the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) relating to the Trust.

On February 14, 2025, Nasdaq and Hashdex jointly launched the Hashdex Nasdaq Crypto Index US ETF. The NCIQ is the first multi-asset spot crypto exchange-traded product (ETP) available to U.S. investors, providing exposure to both spot Bitcoin and Ethereum. Marcelo Sampaio, Co-Founder and CEO of Hashdex, expressed enthusiasm for the NCIQ launch, stating that the firm is thrilled to bring its expertise in crypto index and crypto index-based products to U.S. investors.

The stock market's recent move to recategorize the NCIQ coincides with the Brazilian regulators' approval of Hashdex's XRP ETF application, signaling the launch of Brazil's first spot XRP ETF. Amid increasing institutional interest in altcoin ETFs, Nasdaq has filed 19b-4 forms with the SEC to introduce CoinShares' XRP and Litecoin ETFs. The stock market intends to list and trade the altcoin ETFs of asset manager CoinShares. The US SEC has acknowledged CoinShares' XRP ETF application, marking its fourth recognition of an XRP ETF proposal.

data.title: Short Sellers Lose $73 Billion Amid Market Rally to Start 2025
data.description:
As the new year kicked off, short sellers found themselves on the losing end of a significant market rally, with losses totaling a staggering $73 billion. This sharp reversal in fortunes comes after a year marked by heavy losses for short sellers, who have been grappling with a bullish market and a resurgence in investor confidence.

The rally, which has left the S&P 500 index on track for its strongest month since July 2020, was sparked by a combination of factors. Chief among them was the perceived reluctance of the Federal Reserve to tighten monetary policy any further, following a series of rate hikes in 2024. This sentiment was reinforced by weaker-than-expected US consumer price inflation data released on Tuesday, which gave stocks a further boost.

The upswing triggered a brutal "short squeeze," in which hedge funds repurchased stocks to cover their negative bets, driving share prices even higher. This phenomenon, in which short sellers are forced to buy back the shares they have sold short to limit their losses, has been a significant factor in the recent market rally.



The past month's rally has been particularly painful for short sellers, with many funds suffering significant losses. According to data from S3 Partners, a financial analytics firm, short sellers incurred losses of $43.2 billion on US and European stocks between Tuesday and Friday last week alone. This marks a significant turnaround from the gains that short sellers had made earlier in the year, as the market had been more volatile and less bullish.

The losses have been particularly acute in certain sectors and stocks. For example, short sellers who had bet against technology, healthcare, and consumer discretionary stocks have been among the hardest hit. The share price of cruise line Carnival Corp, for instance, rose by 14% in the week to Monday, costing hedge fund short sellers a total of $240 million.



Meanwhile, heavily shorted Swedish real estate company Samhällsbyggnadsbolaget (SBB) rebounded by a third in the week to Monday, hitting funds including Samlyn Capital, Balyasny, and Arrowstreet Capital. Castellum, whose shares are shorted by Two Creeks Capital, Arrowstreet, and Fosse Capital, jumped 16% this month.

The fate of short sellers has shifted rapidly, and they are now facing a problem. As the market continues to rally, short sellers may be forced to cover their positions at increasingly higher prices, exacerbating their losses. This could lead to a vicious cycle, as the market rally feeds on itself, driving share prices even higher and making it even more difficult for short sellers to cover their positions.

However, it is important to note that short selling can still be a profitable strategy, particularly in a volatile market. By carefully selecting stocks and managing risk, short sellers can still make money, even in a bullish market. Moreover, the recent losses incurred by short sellers may present an opportunity for investors to buy stocks at discounted prices, as the market corrects and short sellers are forced to cover their positions.

In conclusion, the recent market rally has been particularly challenging for short sellers, who have incurred significant losses. However, the market remains volatile, and there are still opportunities for investors to make money, even in a bullish market. By staying informed and managing risk, investors can navigate the challenges posed by short squeezes and other market dynamics, and emerge as winners in the long run.

data.title: Is Exxon Mobil (XOM) the Best Very Cheap Stock to Buy Right Now?
data.description:
Exxon Mobil (XOM) has long been a staple in the energy sector, and its recent performance has raised questions about whether it is the best very cheap stock to buy right now. With a market capitalization of over $480 billion, Exxon Mobil is one of the largest publicly traded companies in the world. Its stock price has been volatile in recent years, but it has shown signs of recovery in 2024. As of February 19, 2025, Exxon Mobil's stock price is around $108.89, which is a significant discount compared to its 52-week high of $126.34.

Exxon Mobil's valuation metrics suggest that it may be undervalued compared to its peers and historical averages. Its trailing PE ratio is 14.07, which is lower than its historical average of around 17. Its forward PE ratio is 14.69, indicating that the market expects the company's earnings to grow in the future. Exxon Mobil's P/S ratio is 1.38, which is lower than its historical average of around 1.5. Its P/B ratio is 1.82, which is lower than its historical average of around 2. These valuation metrics suggest that Exxon Mobil's stock price may be relatively cheap compared to its fundamentals.

Exxon Mobil's dividend history and yield are also attractive. The company has a current annual dividend of $3.96, which amounts to a dividend yield of 3.59%. This yield is higher than the average yield of other energy stocks, which is around 2.5% to 3%. Exxon Mobil has also increased its dividend for 44 consecutive years, which is a testament to its financial stability and growth. The company's dividend payout ratio is 50.51%, which is lower than the industry average of around 60%. This indicates that Exxon Mobil is retaining a significant portion of its earnings for reinvestment, which is a positive sign for future growth.

However, Exxon Mobil faces several risks and challenges in the near and long term. The energy transition and climate change regulations pose a significant threat to the company's core business. Stricter regulations and policies could lead to reduced demand for fossil fuels, impacting Exxon Mobil's revenue and profitability. Commodity price volatility, geopolitical risks, technological disruption, regulatory and legal challenges, and operational risks could also impact the company's stock price. To mitigate these risks and challenges, Exxon Mobil should diversify its business model, invest in renewable energy and carbon capture and storage technologies, strengthen its risk management and operational safety measures, engage with stakeholders, and maintain a strong balance sheet and liquidity position.

In conclusion, Exxon Mobil's valuation metrics, dividend history, and yield suggest that it may be the best very cheap stock to buy right now. However, the company faces several risks and challenges that could impact its stock price. Investors should carefully consider these factors before making a final investment decision. By effectively managing these risks and challenges, Exxon Mobil can enhance its long-term prospects and maintain a stable stock price.

data.title: Alibaba Q4 Earnings Preview: AI Growth, Apple Partnership, and Market Expectations
data.description:
Alibaba Group (BABA) is set to report its highly anticipated quarterly earnings on Thursday before the market opens, with analysts expecting earnings per share (EPS) of $2.69 and revenue of $38.45 billion. This comes amid a strong rally in Alibaba shares, which have surged nearly 50% year-to-date, driven by enthusiasm over its artificial intelligence (AI) initiatives, a newly announced partnership with Apple, and a shift in sentiment following co-founder Jack Ma’s recent meeting with Chinese President Xi Jinping.

A key area to watch in the earnings report is Alibaba’s cloud segment, particularly its advancements in AI, including its collaboration with DeepSeek and its proprietary AI model, Qwen 2.5-Max. Analysts believe Alibaba’s AI developments could play a major role in its long-term growth trajectory. Additionally, the partnership with Apple to integrate Alibaba’s AI capabilities into iPhone features in China could provide a significant boost to its cloud and AI businesses. These initiatives have positioned Alibaba as a leading player in China’s AI infrastructure, but execution will be critical in determining long-term market adoption.

Despite the recent stock rally, Alibaba remains relatively undervalued compared to peers like Amazon and Sea Limited. Its forward P/E ratio of 10.90 is well below the industry average of 32.41, indicating potential upside if it continues to deliver strong results. Additionally, Alibaba’s price-to-sales (P/S) and price-to-book (P/B) ratios remain attractive relative to competitors, reinforcing the case that the stock is cheap compared to its long-term growth potential. However, given the sharp rise in share price, investors will be looking for strong guidance and performance to sustain recent gains.

Alibaba’s e-commerce business remains its core revenue driver, and analysts will be watching whether it can maintain its market share amid growing competition from PDD Holdings and JD.com. Additionally, management’s commentary on international expansion, particularly through its integration of domestic Taobao and Tmall with its global marketplace, will be key. Growth in its advertising and logistics businesses will also be critical indicators of future profitability, as these high-margin segments have been a major focus for the company.

On the macroeconomic front, Alibaba has faced challenges due to China’s uneven economic recovery, regulatory scrutiny, and geopolitical tensions. However, recent policy shifts by the Chinese government, including efforts to restore private sector confidence, could provide a tailwind. The presence of Jack Ma at a recent symposium with Xi has fueled speculation that regulatory pressures may ease, potentially benefiting Alibaba and other Chinese tech giants. Investors will closely monitor management’s tone regarding regulatory concerns and government support for tech sector growth.

Looking ahead, Alibaba’s results will be a key test of whether the company can sustain its strong rally and reassure investors about its growth trajectory. The market will be looking for positive guidance on AI monetization, e-commerce stability, and margin expansion in its cloud business. With options traders pricing in significant volatility, Alibaba’s stock reaction post-earnings will largely depend on whether the company meets or exceeds expectations in these critical areas. If Alibaba delivers on AI momentum and continued business expansion, it could reinforce bullish sentiment and push shares higher.

data.title: Fed's Delicate Balance: Rates, Inflation, and Tariffs
data.description:
The Federal Reserve (Fed) finds itself in a precarious position as it navigates the delicate balance between interest rates, inflation, and the impact of tariffs on the U.S. economy. With inflation running above its target and the looming threat of tariffs, the Fed must carefully consider its next move to maintain price stability and support maximum employment.



Inflation and Interest Rates

The Fed's primary tool for managing inflation is the federal funds rate, the interest rate at which banks lend to each other overnight. When the Fed determines that inflation is too high, it typically raises the federal funds rate to slow down economic activity and reduce inflation. However, raising interest rates too aggressively can also slow down economic growth and lead to job losses, which is why the Fed must carefully balance its dual mandate of maximum employment and price stability.

Tariffs and Inflation

Tariffs can have a significant impact on inflation by increasing the cost of imported goods and services. When the U.S. imposes tariffs on imported goods, the prices of those goods typically increase, which can lead to higher inflation. Additionally, tariffs can disrupt supply chains and lead to further price increases as businesses struggle to source alternative suppliers or adjust their production processes.

The Fed must consider the potential impact of tariffs on inflation when making its monetary policy decisions. If tariffs lead to a significant and sustained increase in inflation, the Fed may need to raise interest rates to bring inflation back down to its target of 2%. However, if the tariffs have a more muted impact on inflation or lead to significant job losses, the Fed may need to keep interest rates lower to support employment growth.



Navigating the Challenges

The Fed faces a challenging task in balancing its dual mandate in the face of tariff-induced inflation. It must carefully consider the potential impacts of tariffs on both inflation and employment, use forward guidance to manage market expectations, and adjust its policy rate as needed to maintain price stability and support maximum employment.

In conclusion, the Fed is in a precarious spot as it balances its dual mandate of maximum employment and price stability in the face of tariff-induced inflation. By carefully considering the potential impacts of tariffs on both inflation and employment, using forward guidance to manage market expectations, and adjusting its policy rate as needed, the Fed can navigate this challenging landscape and maintain economic stability and growth.

data.title: Bitcoin and Stablecoins: Revolutionizing Global Finance and GDP Growth
data.description:
Bitcoin's potential for global payment scalability has been a hot topic in recent discussions, with industry experts exploring how innovations like stablecoins could reshape international financial dynamics. In a recent episode of The Scoop, Lightspark Co-Founder and CEO David Marcus shared his insights on how stablecoins and Bitcoin could revolutionize global finance and GDP growth.

Marcus highlighted the transformative potential of integrating stablecoins with Bitcoin, stating that it could not only streamline transactions but also positively impact GDP globally. He emphasized the importance of enhancing Bitcoin's transaction speed and efficiency to achieve widespread adoption as a mainstream currency.

One core challenge identified is the inherent scalability issues linked to Bitcoin's current blockchain system. Transactions can often take longer and cost more during peak times, which hinders its effectiveness as a true currency for everyday transactions. Marcus noted that finding the right balance between decentralization and scalability is critical to Bitcoin meeting the demands of a global economy.

The conversation took a significant turn when stablecoins were introduced as a potential solution to some of Bitcoin's scalability issues. Marcus explained that stablecoins can provide the necessary liquidity for transactions while leveraging Bitcoin's security features, offering a combined approach for efficiency and trust. This integration could serve as a bridge, effectively allowing Bitcoin to operate as a settlement layer for stablecoin transfers.

As the regulatory environment evolves, Marcus pointed out the historical hurdles faced by the crypto industry regarding compliance and how these have shaped current market dynamics. However, he expressed optimism about the changing regulatory landscape, suggesting that governments are becoming more receptive to cryptocurrency innovation.

Looking ahead, the potential impact that stablecoins on Bitcoin could have on global GDP is startling. With enhanced speed and lower transaction costs, their adoption could lead to significant economic benefits, unlocking new avenues for commerce and transaction methods. Marcus envisions a future where businesses and consumers could interact seamlessly through this integrated system, boosting economic activities worldwide.

As developments continue, stakeholders in the crypto space must keep a close eye on these advancements that could redefine transaction methodologies and drive economic growth. The insights from David Marcus on the strategic growth of Bitcoin and the role of stablecoins provide valuable perspectives for stakeholders looking to understand the future of payments.

data.title: SEC Halts Crypto Enforcement Cases Amidst Leadership Transition
data.description:
The U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) has reportedly been prioritizing the pause of cryptocurrency enforcement cases with imminent deadlines, according to Fox Business reporter Eleanor Terrett. This pause is partly why the securities regulator has not yet halted its lawsuits against crypto firms Ripple and Kraken.

The next court deadline for Ripple, the developer of the XRP Ledger blockchain network, is April 16. Meanwhile, cryptocurrency exchange Kraken's next deadline is March 31. Coinbase and Binance do not face court deadlines until March 14 and April 14, respectively.

It is possible that SEC leadership is expecting the confirmation of Paul Atkins, former SEC commissioner and President Trump's pick for chair, by the time these deadlines approach. In the interim, the crypto task force, Congress, and the Presidential Working Group on Digital Assets are presumably working to fill the regulatory gaps that led to these lawsuits.

Under former U.S. President Joe Biden, financial regulators, namely the SEC and the Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC), brought more than 100 lawsuits against crypto firms for alleged legal violations. In 2023, the SEC sued Kraken, alleging that the exchange operated as an unregistered securities broker. The agency also sued Coinbase for purportedly failing to register as a broker, national securities exchange, or clearing agency, thus evading the disclosure scheme for securities markets.

The agency previously took action against these exchanges for offering cryptocurrency staking services in the U.S. Ripple has been mired in a lawsuit with the SEC since 2020, with the regulator alleging that Ripple issued unregistered securities when launching its XRP (XRP) token.

By contrast, U.S. President Donald Trump has taken an industry-friendly approach in his second term, promising to cut back on enforcement actions against crypto firms and make America "the world's crypto capital." Trump has nominated Paul Atkins, a former SEC commissioner, to chair the SEC. The agency has been gradually winding down or pausing enforcement actions against crypto firms. On Jan. 30, 10 days after Trump began his second presidential term, Kraken resumed staking services for U.S. clients for the first time in nearly two years.

data.title: Montana Moves to Invest $50M in Bitcoin
data.description:
Montana's legislature has taken a significant step towards embracing cryptocurrency by advancing a bill that would allocate $50 million for a strategic Bitcoin reserve. The legislation, House Bill 317, has now moved to the House vote stage, marking a crucial milestone in the state's potential investment in the world's most popular cryptocurrency.

The bill, introduced by Representative Alan Doane, aims to establish a strategic reserve of Bitcoin to diversify Montana's investment portfolio and potentially generate revenue for the state. If passed, Montana would become the first U.S. state to invest in Bitcoin directly, setting a precedent for other states to follow suit.

The proposed investment of $50 million represents a significant opportunity for Montana to capitalize on the growing acceptance and potential of cryptocurrencies. Bitcoin, in particular, has gained widespread recognition and has shown remarkable resilience despite market fluctuations. The state's investment could yield substantial returns, given Bitcoin's historical performance and its increasing adoption by institutional investors.

However, the bill's passage is not guaranteed, and it faces potential challenges in the House vote. Some lawmakers may express concerns about the volatility of cryptocurrencies and the potential risks associated with investing in Bitcoin. Additionally, the bill's success will depend on the support it receives from Montana's House members, who will ultimately decide its fate.

If House Bill 317 is approved, Montana will join a growing list of governments and institutions that have recognized the potential of cryptocurrencies and have begun investing in them. This trend reflects the increasing acceptance and integration of cryptocurrencies into the global financial landscape.

The potential investment by Montana in Bitcoin could also have broader implications for the cryptocurrency industry. It may encourage other states and governments to consider similar investments, further legitimizing cryptocurrencies and fostering their growth. Moreover, the investment could attract more institutional investors, leading to increased liquidity and stability in the cryptocurrency market.

In conclusion, Montana's strategic Bitcoin reserve legislation represents a significant opportunity for the state to diversify its investment portfolio and capitalize on the potential of cryptocurrencies. As the bill moves to the House vote stage, the fate of this innovative proposal rests in the hands of Montana's lawmakers. If approved, Montana could become a trailblazer in the world of cryptocurrency investments, setting a precedent for other states and institutions to follow.

data.title: Ethereum's 2025 Price Rally Hinges on $2,800 Break
data.description:
Ethereum's potential price rally in 2025 hinges on a key resistance break above $2,800, according to market analysts. As the cryptocurrency approaches a critical juncture, historical trends suggest a potential rally could emerge by the end of March 2025. Recent developments surrounding Solana's LIBRA memecoin incident have shifted investor sentiment favorably towards Ethereum, which may propel its price higher.

Ethereum has shown a consistent pattern of recovery during the second half of the first quarter in recent years. Analysts note that since 2020, Ether has historically experienced strong rebounds in this timeframe, independent of broader market conditions. According to Honey, a full-time crypto trader, the token has achieved an impressive average increase of around 40% in the final six weeks of Q1, suggesting a potential price resurgence leading to targets around $3,500, contingent on the market dynamics holding up.

Honey emphasizes the necessity for Ethereum's price to break through key resistance levels soon, or risk his bullish outlook being proven wrong. The price trajectory has led to the formation of lower highs and a consolidation period below key resistance ranges between $2,800 and $2,850. To prevent echoing the earlier price stagnation, Ethereum urgently needs to establish consistent daily closes above this resistance spectrum. Failure to do so could prompt a softer market response, potentially re-testing supports below $2,300, thus raising concerns about its viability heading into the later part of the year.

Ethereum's future also hinges on broader market trends, especially given the increasing number of altcoins vying for investor attention. While Ethereum is currently lagging behind its historical performance metrics, analysts remain optimistic about the potential for recovery should external conditions favor a bullish shift. data.title: Chase Tightens Zelle Rules Amid Fraud Fears
data.description:
JPMorgan Chase, one of the largest banks in the United States, has announced significant changes to the way its customers can use the popular payments service Zelle. The move comes in response to a growing concern over fraud and scams, which have cost customers of Chase, Bank of America, and Wells Fargo a combined total of $456 million in just one year.

The new restrictions, set to take effect next month, will block Zelle payments that appear to be connected to social media contacts and online marketplaces. Chase has stated that the Zelle service should be used for payments between friends, family, and others whom the sender trusts, and not for purchasing goods from recipients with whom they are unfamiliar.

In an effort to further protect customers, Chase may directly ask customers what payments are for and how they contacted the recipient. The bank will block transactions identified as potentially risky, and may also restrict a customer's use of Zelle or take other actions if they engage in risky behavior or provide false information about their payments.

Last year, the Senate's Permanent Subcommittee on Investigations found that customers at Chase, Wells Fargo, and Bank of America submitted claims to recover $456 million in 2022, citing fraud and scams on Zelle. The banks reimbursed a total of $341 million for these claims.

Zelle's parent company, Early Warning Services, which is owned by seven major US banks, including the three financial giants mentioned above, maintains that less than one-tenth of one percent of transactions are reported as fraud or scams.

In December, the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau (CFPB) sued Early Warning Services (EWS), Chase, Wells Fargo, and Bank of America, alleging that they failed to implement adequate safeguards against fraud on Zelle. EWS and the banks have denied the allegations, arguing that Zelle has strong anti-fraud measures in place.

The changes announced by JPMorgan Chase aim to better protect customers from fraud and scams, and to ensure that the Zelle service is used as intended. As the use of digital payments continues to grow, it is essential for financial institutions to remain vigilant and adapt their security measures to address emerging threats.

data.title: BlackRock Stock: What's Happening Today?
data.description:
Alright, let's dive into what's going on with BlackRock stock today. As an avid investor, I'm always on the lookout for companies that are making waves in the market, and BlackRock is definitely one of them. So, let's break down the latest news and data to see what's happening.

First things first, BlackRock just released its Q4 2024 earnings report, and it's looking pretty darn impressive. The company reported an EPS of $8.57, up 5% from last quarter's $8.17. That's a solid increase, and it shows that BlackRock is continuing to grow its profitability. Total revenue reached $4.3 billion, representing a +8% QoQ revenue growth. That's some serious cash coming in, and it's a clear indication that BlackRock's business is booming.

Now, let's talk about the elephant in the room: BlackRock's valuation gap. As the company's earnings growth and stock price appreciation have aligned, the valuation gap has narrowed. This is reflected in the average target price for BlackRock stock, which is $1,129, representing a +14.44% increase from the latest price. That's a significant jump, and it shows that analysts are bullish on BlackRock's future prospects.

But what's driving this narrowing valuation gap and stock price appreciation? Well, it all comes down to BlackRock's strategic market positioning and a surge in demand for its ETF products amid market volatility. The company has continued to leverage its competitive advantage in the ETF industry, resulting in a substantial increase in revenue. This strategic positioning, coupled with the strong demand for ETFs, has allowed BlackRock to capitalize on market trends and attract more investors to its ETF products.

Now, let's take a look at some data to illustrate BlackRock's impressive performance. Check out this chart that shows the company's revenue growth over the past few years:

data.title: TPG RE Finance Trust's Q4 2024: Navigating Contradictions in Leverage Strategy, CMBS Activity, and Investment Focus
data.description:
These are the key contradictions discussed in TPG RE Finance Trust's latest 2024Q4 earnings call, specifically including: Leverage Strategy and REO Portfolio Management, CMBS Market Activity, and Multifamily and Life Sciences Investment Focus:



Strong Economic Sentiment and Earnings Performance:
- TRTX reported strong economic growth and a resilient labor market driving U.S. economic power, with the Fed pausing potential rate cuts in 2025.
- The company's earnings reflect successful strategy execution, maintaining a 100% performing balance sheet, stable credit risk ratings, and consistent reductions in CECL reserves.

Loan Investment Activity and Growth:
- TRTX increased net earning assets by 3% in Q4 due to $446 million in new loan commitments, primarily in multifamily and industrial sectors with an LTV of approximately 60% and a weighted-average spread of SOFR plus 3.25%.
- The company expects increased 2024 loan investment volume to accelerate in 2025, driven by its offensively oriented balance sheet and robust sourcing channels.

Strategic Asset Management and Risk Mitigation:
- TRTX foreclosed on two multifamily loans during Q4 and immediately took operational control, aiming to stabilize and prepare properties for sale.
- The company emphasized strategic asset management and risk mitigation, with a focus on abiding by commercially reasonable terms in loan modifications and extensions to maximize shareholder value.

Liquidity and Financing Capacity:
- TRTX has significant liquidity of $320.8 million, with leverage at 2.14:1, allowing for new loan investment activity without relying solely on loan repayments.
- The company anticipates strong growth in earning assets in 2025, supported by its existing financing capacity and potential participation in the CRE CLO market to optimize capital markets.

Dividend Yield and Share Repurchase:
- TRTX generates an 11% dividend yield, supported by a low leverage ratio, high liquidity, and a 100% performing loan portfolio.
- The company continues its share repurchase plan alongside new investments, leveraging its liquidity and financing capacity to drive earnings and shareholder value.

data.title: Trump's Tariffs and Deportations: Fed Warns of Higher Inflation
data.description:
Federal Reserve officials have warned that President Trump's tariffs and migrant deportations could contribute to higher inflation this year. In a recent hearing before the House Financial Services Committee, Fed Chairman Jerome Powell testified that these policies could have significant impacts on the U.S. economy and consumer prices.

Powell noted that the tariffs imposed by the Trump administration on various goods, including steel and aluminum, have raised production costs for U.S. manufacturers. This, in turn, could lead to higher prices for consumers as companies pass on these increased costs. Additionally, Powell expressed concern about the potential impact of migrant deportations on the labor market and the economy.

The Fed chairman emphasized that the central bank is closely monitoring these developments and will adjust monetary policy as needed to maintain price stability and support maximum employment. However, Powell also acknowledged that the Fed's ability to offset the effects of these policies is limited, and that the ultimate impact on inflation will depend on various factors, including the extent to which companies pass on higher costs to consumers and the response of other countries to U.S. trade policies.

Economists and analysts have also weighed in on the potential impact of Trump's policies on inflation. Some have argued that the tariffs could lead to higher prices for consumers, particularly for goods that are heavily reliant on imported materials. Others have noted that the deportations could lead to labor shortages in certain sectors, driving up wages and potentially increasing inflation.

However, some experts have also expressed skepticism about the extent to which these policies will actually impact inflation. They argue that the overall impact of the tariffs and deportations on the economy is likely to be relatively modest, and that other factors, such as changes in consumer demand and productivity, are more likely to drive inflation in the long run.

In any case, the Fed's warnings about the potential impact of Trump's policies on inflation highlight the complex interplay between trade, immigration, and monetary policy. As the U.S. economy continues to evolve, the Fed will need to remain vigilant in monitoring these developments and adjusting its policies as needed to maintain price stability and support maximum employment.

data.title: Bitcoin Rewards App Fold Makes Nasdaq Debut
data.description:
Bitcoin rewards app Fold has made its public debut, commencing trading on the Nasdaq Composite on Wednesday. The company, which offers Bitcoin rewards and financial services, began trading under the ticker symbol FLD at $10.25 per share, subsequently rising to over $12.

Fold CEO Will Reeves expressed excitement about the listing on Nasdaq, stating that the company looks forward to continuing its mission to expand access to Bitcoin rewards and financial services as a public entity. Fold is now trading on Nasdaq under the symbol FLD.

Fold is one of the few digital asset companies to be publicly traded. Last year, the company announced plans to go public via a special purpose acquisition company (SPAC) agreement with FTAC Emerald Acquisition Corp., valuing the firm at $365 million.

Since its inception in 2019, Fold has processed over $2 billion in volume through services such as bill pay, debit cards, and insured accounts. Customers accrue Bitcoin rewards by utilizing Fold's services and can purchase BTC through the platform without fees. To date, Fold has distributed over $20 million worth of BTC rewards, valued at approximately $70 million due to the cryptocurrency's price appreciation.

In a statement, Fold CEO Will Reeves highlighted the company's commitment to expanding access to wealth creation for its clients. Fold has partnered with Visa to release a rewards debit card and is currently developing a credit card that will allow clients to "spend USD on credit and stack sats."

Other Bitcoin-focused companies trading on the Nasdaq include American miners like RIOT and software company Strategy (formerly MicroStrategy), which has rebranded as a "Bitcoin treasury company."

data.title: "TAO Soars 17% on Coinbase Listing, Sparking AI Token Revival"
data.description:
Bittensor (TAO), a prominent AI token, has witnessed a remarkable 17% price surge following its listing on Coinbase, exemplifying the influential "Coinbase Effect" on the crypto market. This surge comes amidst ongoing volatility in the broader AI market, yet TAO's performance demonstrates resilience and potential post-listing.

The listing of Bittensor (TAO) on Coinbase has sparked significant investor interest and trading volume, reflecting the exchange's substantial sway over market trends. Historically, when a new token is listed on Coinbase, it characteristically experiences a surge in investor interest and trading volume, a phenomenon known as the "Coinbase Effect." This trend has continued into 2025, demonstrating its persistent relevance.

Following the announcement of Bittensor's listing, the token experienced an impressive jump of over 17%. This immediate reaction underscores the potent influence Coinbase retains over the cryptocurrency landscape. According to Coinbase's social media channels, trading of Bittensor (TAO) on the Bittensor network began on or after 9 AM PT on February 20, 2025, if liquidity conditions were met. Once a sufficient supply of this asset was established, trading on the TAO-USD trading pair launched in phases, further exciting investors and traders alike.

Bittensor, an integral player in the AI token space, has experienced a turbulent price journey over recent weeks, reflecting broader trends affecting AI-related cryptocurrencies. After a hearty period in late January, the altcoin endured a significant downturn in early February, primarily attributed to significant market liquidations. Despite these struggles, the combination of Coinbase's strategic listing roadmap and the renewed attention towards AI tokens, driven by high-profile figures such as Elon Musk, is fostering a recovery of TAO's token value. Currently, the altcoin stands as one of the most searched entities on crypto tracking platforms, a testament to its rising fame and market interest.

The surge in Bittensor's price is not merely an isolated event; it signals a potential rebound for AI tokens as a whole. Bittensor's scenario, marked by fluctuating prices, mirrors the overall climate in which AI cryptocurrency exists. The market previously suffered setbacks, notably due to events surrounding AI firm DeepSeek, but recent developments suggest a possible revival. With the increasing engagement of notable investors and entities in AI

data.title: Precision BioSciences' Gene Editing Therapy Shows Promise in Early Hepatitis B Trial
data.description:
Precision BioSciences, Inc. (DTIL) has announced encouraging initial safety and antiviral activity data from its Phase 1 dose-finding study, ELIMINATE-B, for chronic Hepatitis B. The trial is evaluating PBGENE-HBV, a novel in vivo gene editing therapy designed to directly eliminate and inactivate the root cause of Hepatitis B virus from covalently closed circular DNA (cccDNA) and integrated DNA.

PBGENE-HBV demonstrated a strong safety profile in the first cohort of patients, with no Grade ≥2 treatment-related adverse events or serious adverse events reported. This is a significant achievement, as existing hepatitis B treatments often come with side effects and potential long-term complications. The well-tolerated safety profile of PBGENE-HBV, combined with its early antiviral activity, suggests that it could potentially offer a more favorable risk-benefit profile compared to existing treatments.

In addition to safety, PBGENE-HBV demonstrated substantial reduction in Hepatitis B surface antigen (HBsAg) in two of the three participants following the first administration at the lowest dose level (0.2 mg/kg). This early antiviral activity supports the potential of the drug to eliminate the root cause of Hepatitis B virus from cccDNA and integrated DNA, which is crucial for achieving undetectable levels of HBsAg.

The planned dosing schedule in the ELIMINATE-B trial balances the need for safety and efficacy by allowing for two additional administrations at the lowest dose level while simultaneously investigating the next higher dose level. This approach enables Precision BioSciences to gather more data on the safety and antiviral activity of PBGENE-HBV at the initial dose level before escalating to higher doses.



As the ELIMINATE-B study progresses, additional administrations at higher dose levels will help define the optimal dose and number of dose administrations for safely eliminating cccDNA and inactivating integrated HBV DNA. This will further validate the drug's efficacy and potential for treating chronic Hepatitis B in a broader patient population.

The substantial reduction in HBsAg observed in two of the three participants can be attributed to several factors, including PBGENE-HBV's unique mechanism of action, the dose level and administration, patient characteristics, and consistency with preclinical data. These factors suggest that PBGENE-HBV may be effective across a range of chronic Hepatitis B patient profiles.

In conclusion, the encouraging initial safety and antiviral activity data from the ELIMINATE-B trial support the potential of PBGENE-HBV as a novel and effective treatment option for chronic Hepatitis B. As the study continues, investors will eagerly await further data to validate the drug's efficacy and potential market adoption.
